commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider-aware Codex app-server approval reviewer selection across run attempts, bound turns, side-question forks, and an internal exec auto-review bridge for local/custom providers.

PR surface: Source +348, Tests +790, Generated 0. Total +1138 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can force auto_review before provider scoping, and the PR's remaining user-only requirements failure is also source-reproducible without a live LM Studio round trip.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is draft, dirty, maintainer-protected, and security-sensitive; it needs author or owner repair for requirements validation plus explicit approval-boundary acceptance.

target.title: fix(codex): avoid guardian review for local models
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Prevents Codex app-server Guardian / `auto_review` from being used as the approval reviewer when the active model provider is local or custom, such as LM Studio.
- Keeps Codex/OpenAI app-server runs on the native model-backed approval reviewer path.
- Routes local/custom Codex app-server command approvals through OpenClaw's own `tools.exec.mode=auto` reviewer when the app-server reviewer has been downgraded to `user`.
- Applies the same provider-aware reviewer policy to normal Codex app-server runs, bound conversation turns, native code-mode-only request shaping, and side-question forks.

Why does this matter now?

- `tools.exec.mode=auto` forced Codex Guardian before checking the selected model provider. A local model routed through the Codex harness could therefore be treated as if Codex Guardian could safely review it.
- If the user configured an OpenAI reviewer model for exec auto-review while running a local/custom main model, the app-server path previously had no internal OpenClaw reviewer bridge after Guardian was disabled.

What is the intended outcome?

- Codex-native and OpenAI-backed Codex app-server runs can keep native `auto_review`.
- Local/custom providers fall back to `approvalsReviewer: "user"` while retaining `approvalPolicy: "on-request"` and workspace sandboxing.
- When `tools.exec.mode=auto` is active, command approvals can be reviewed by OpenClaw's configured exec reviewer model through the internal simple-completion harness, including the existing default behavior where omitted `tools.exec.reviewer.model` reuses the current agent model.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to Codex app-server protocol fields or provider catalog configuration.
- No live local-model provider round trip in this PR.

What does success look like?

- `lmstudio` / local provider Codex runs no longer send `approvalsReviewer: "auto_review"` or `guardian_subagent`.
- Existing provider-qualified legacy bindings such as `lmstudio/local-model` are handled safely even without a stored `modelProvider`.
- Local/custom app-server command approvals in exec auto mode either use OpenClaw's internal reviewer or fall back to the existing plugin/human approval route when the request is not safe for model auto-review.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The provider gate in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.ts`.
- The app-server approval bridge in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts`.
- The SDK helper in `src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts` that builds parser-bound exec auto-review input.
- Side-question fork policy resolution in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Maintainer-requested investigation and fix during local debugging.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: local/custom Codex harness model providers no longer use Codex Guardian / native model-backed approval review for exec auto mode; app-server command approvals can instead use OpenClaw's internal exec reviewer model path when safe.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on the Codex plugin test harness, plus Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01kszp8dzjvt2r50r1yw5gdp40` against pushed head `448efb65b377eafb6440ebf1ae1af0e25deab8c1`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/exec-auto-review.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`; `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/exec-auto-review.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`; `git diff --check`; `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts src/agents/exec-auto-reviewer.test.ts src/infra/exec-auto-review.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "set -euo pipefail; env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=true corepack pnpm check:changed"`.
- Evidence after fix: local focused Vitest reported 7 passed files / 204 passed tests; local oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and plugin SDK API baseline check passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; Testbox `check:changed` passed on `tbx_01kszp8dzjvt2r50r1yw5gdp40`.
- Observed result after fix: mocked `lmstudio` Codex app-server start/turn, code-mode-only, side-question, and bound-conversation flows keep prompting approval and sandboxing but send `approvalsReviewer: "user"`; Codex/OpenAI providers still keep `auto_review`; app-server auto command approvals call OpenClaw's exec reviewer only for parser-bound single commands and skip to plugin approval for amendments, restricted approval choices, security-audit suppression edits, unbound shell chains, reviewer `ask`, or run cancellation.
- What was not tested: a live LM Studio or Ollama app-server round trip with a real model process.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: full `pnpm test extensions/codex` was not treated as a PR gate because earlier Blacksmith Testbox proof reproduced current `origin/main` failures in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.test.ts` for legacy auth-provider alias handling. The PR-touched files and changed-gate proof pass.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing config resolution forced `tools.exec.mode=auto` into Codex app-server `approvalsReviewer: "auto_review"` before considering the selected model provider, and app-server approval fallback did not consult OpenClaw's configured exec reviewer model.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/exec-auto-review.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/exec-auto-review.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts src/agents/exec-auto-reviewer.test.ts src/infra/exec-auto-review.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "set -euo pipefail; env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=true corepack pnpm check:changed"`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Policy-level coverage for Codex/OpenAI preserving model-backed review and custom providers downgrading to user review.
- Run-attempt coverage for `lmstudio` + `tools.exec.mode=auto` request shaping.
- Bound-conversation coverage for stored custom `modelProvider` and legacy provider-qualified model refs.
- Native code-mode-only coverage for provider-qualified local Codex model refs on main turns and side-question forks.
- App-server approval bridge coverage for configured/default exec reviewer models, reviewer `ask` fallback, permission/policy amendment fallback, amendment-only decision fallback, unbound shell fallback, security-audit suppression fallback, and abort cancellation.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The runtime policy was selected before model-provider scoping, so `tools.exec.mode=auto` could send model-backed Guardian review for non-OpenAI model providers.
- After Guardian was disabled for those providers, app-server command approvals did not use the configured OpenClaw exec reviewer model path.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Approval routing for Codex app-server exec auto mode when a run uses provider overrides or existing bound-session metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change narrows only model-backed approval review. It preserves prompting approval policy and sandbox posture for custom providers, preserves Codex/OpenAI behavior, runs OpenClaw internal exec review only after native hooks/tool policy checks, and falls back to plugin/human approval for cancellation, unsafe approval shapes, unbound commands, security suppression edits, and reviewer uncertainty.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Live local-model app-server proof was not run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Autoreview completed with no accepted/actionable findings.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the code path and kept the change scoped to Codex app-server approval reviewer routing.
target.author: vincentkoc
